composition_vocabulary: Tiles alternate between close-up object-on-neutral-ground shots and worn/in-context lifestyle shots; negative space is used generously in the product frames while the lifestyle tiles are busier and edge-to-edge. There is no consistent framing language — the board mixes flat-lay, hand-held, and face-obscured portrait orientations freely.
lighting_quality: Predominantly flat, diffused studio or natural light for product tiles; lifestyle tiles introduce harsher, more ambient or mixed lighting — chiaroscuro is absent.
material_textures: polished sterling silver, matte ceramic/resin, raw oxidised metal, velvet/foam presentation lining, fabric/jersey, moulded rubber or silicone
aesthetic_register: The sample sits at the intersection of contemporary art-school craft and streetwear-adjacent jewellery — more Dimes Square or emerging-artist drop than traditional heirloom luxury, with occasional nods to ancient symbolic iconography.
notably_absent: There is almost no precious-stone colour (no diamonds, emeralds, sapphires set in traditional settings), no high-gloss editorial fashion photography, and none of the aspirational lifestyle backdrops — yachts, marble interiors, candlelit tables — typical of mainstream luxury jewellery moodboards.
